title: CONSTRUCTION SUPERVISOR
description:
Job Description & Requirements

A Construction Supervisor with responsibilities for waterproofing, painting, and electrical duties plays a crucial role in overseeing and managing various aspects of a construction project. Here's a breakdown of the job scope for each area:

Waterproofing (Open roof area) :

  • Site Inspection: Conducting thorough inspections of the construction site to identify areas that require waterproofing. This includes assessing the condition of surfaces, joints, potential water entry points, and PU Grouting.
  • Material Selection: Choosing appropriate waterproofing materials based on site conditions, local building codes, and project requirements.
  • Supervision of Application: Overseeing the application of waterproofing materials, ensuring proper techniques are followed to create a seamless and effective barrier against water intrusion.
  • Quality Control: Conducting regular quality checks to ensure that waterproofing work meets industry standards and project specifications.

Painting:

  • Surface Preparation: Ensuring that surfaces to be painted are properly cleaned, repaired, and primed before applying paint. This may involve tasks like sanding, filling cracks, and applying sealants.
  • Color and Material Selection: Assisting in the selection of appropriate paint types, colors, and finishes based on project requirements, design plans, and client preferences.
  • Supervision of Application: Overseeing the painting process to ensure proper techniques are used, including correct mixing of paint, application methods, and drying times.
  • Quality Assurance: Conducting inspections to verify that the painting work meets quality standards and adheres to project specifications.

Electrical Duties:

  • Installation Oversight: Supervising the installation of electrical systems, including wiring, outlets, switches, lighting fixtures, and other electrical components according to approved plans and local building codes.
  • Coordination with Electricians: Working closely with electricians to ensure that electrical work is carried out safely and in compliance with industry standards.
  • Testing and Commissioning: Conducting tests to ensure proper functioning of electrical systems and coordinating with relevant parties for any necessary adjustments or corrections.
  • Safety Compliance: Ensuring that all electrical work is performed in a safe manner, with proper grounding, insulation, and protection measures in place.

General Responsibilities:

  • Team Management: Supervising a team of workers, assigning tasks, and providing guidance and support to ensure that work is carried out efficiently and to the required quality standards.
  • Communication: Maintaining clear and effective communication with other stakeholders, including project managers, architects, subcontractors, and clients.
  • Documentation: Keeping accurate records of work progress, materials used, and any incidents or issues that arise during the construction process.

In addition to these specific duties, a Construction Supervisor in this role will also be responsible for ensuring compliance with safety regulations, managing schedules, and coordinating with other trades to ensure a smooth and successful construction process. They may also be involved in budget management, procurement of materials, and problem-solving as issues arise on-site. On-site will majorily be outdoors under the sun.

Minimum of 3 years experience required.
